summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that JustHTML parses HTML with thousands of entity references without enforcing limits on entity count, expansion size, or processing time. Resource consumption (CPU time and memory) scales linearly with entity count, demonstrating that an attacker can exhaust server resources by sending HTML with millions of entities.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should parse HTML with progressively larger entity counts (1,000 to 50,000 entities) and demonstrate linear scaling of CPU time and memory consumption without any limits, proving that resource exhaustion DoS is achievable.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image and executed the exploit script. The script successfully parsed HTML with 1,000, 5,000, 10,000, 25,000, and 50,000 entity references, measuring parse time and memory delta for each. Results confirmed linear scaling: 1,000 entities processed in ~0.7ms with 10KB memory; 50,000 entities processed in ~17ms with 488KB memory. No limits were enforced on entity count, expansion size, or processing time.
verification_method: The exploit script measures parse time and memory consumption for progressively larger entity counts, then analyzes scaling behavior. Linear scaling (time and memory proportional to entity count) without limits confirms the DoS vulnerability. The script projects impact for 1,000,000 entities (estimated 350ms, 9.8MB memory) to demonstrate attack feasibility.
evidence_produced_by_run: Successfully parsed HTML with 1,000 entities in 0.7ms, consuming 10KB memory
Successfully parsed HTML with 50,000 entities in 17ms, consuming 488KB memory
Confirmed linear scaling: time and memory scale proportionally with entity count
No entity count limit enforced: processed 50,000 entities without rejection
No expansion size limit enforced: consumed 488KB memory for entity decoding
No timeout enforced: parsing continued until completion without deadline
Projected impact for 1,000,000 entities: ~350ms CPU time, ~9.8MB memory - feasible DoS
Attack vector confirmed: downstream applications can be exploited by sending malicious HTML over network

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the target repository from the session directory (${SESSION_DIR}/repo) and mounts it read-only into the container at /target-repo.
